$comment: Pack definitions — the source of truth for what ships beyond the core bundle.
Adding a pack: add an entry here, add test/fixtures/packs/<id>.tex that uses it, then
run `pnpm bundle:packs`. The fixture is not optional: presence in a manifest is not
evidence a package works, which is exactly how siunitx once shipped listed-but-broken.
See PACKS.md for the design and the invariants.

packs.label: Writing & page layout
packs.description: Headers, footers, section styling, line spacing and title pages.

packs.label: Figures & placement
packs.description: Figure positioning, text wrapping, subfigures and PDF inclusion.

packs.label: Coloured boxes
packs.description: tcolorbox — framed, coloured and breakable boxes for notes and theorems.

packs.label: Annotation & review
packs.description: Margin notes, highlighting, strikethrough and cancelled maths.

packs.label: Science notation
packs.description: Physics vector notation and chemical formulae.

packs.label: Advanced tables
packs.description: Matrix-style and modern table layouts beyond the core table support.

packs.label: Citation styles
packs.description: natbib author-year citations and csquotes context-sensitive quoting. Note: generating a bibliography still needs BibTeX or Biber, which cannot run in the browser.

packs.label: Drafting helpers
packs.description: Placeholder text and flexible list customisation for drafts.
